summary: Matched more of player bonus library code, centered on plbonuslib. The PR added full matches for pl_8003D644 and pl_8003ED0C and completed matches for fn_8003EE2C and pl_8004049C, with the decomp.dev bot reporting +4096 bytes and +0.11% matched code for GALE01. Supporting changes refined fighter/player structs, exposed pl_040D prototypes, replaced the obsolete pl_0371 include, and corrected several function signatures and symbol string ranges needed by the match.
what_changed: Implemented/matched pl_8003D644 in plbonuslib.c, a large player bonus accounting routine that reads fighter hit/attack metadata, updates stale move table fields, awards many bonus IDs via pl_80038824/pl_80038788, and handles attacker/victim/team conditions.
Added matched pl_8003ED0C with an extra int parameter in the header, updating average/total fields in the stale move table and using the plbonusinline.h assert on nonzero total.
Reworked fn_8003EE2C enough to complete matching: simplified temporaries, introduced helper predicates, used GA_Air/GA_Ground names, normalized increments/decrements, and scoped item-gobj handling.
Changed pl_8003D60C from int to bool and returned true/false explicitly for its item/action ID predicate.
Added inline helper functions in plbonuslib.c such as gm_8016B168_and_same_team, unk_cond, between_A1_D0, match_item_kind, and gm_cond to express repeated conditions in a match-friendly way.
Introduced struct UnkPlBonusBits in ft/types.h and changed Fighter.dmg.x18d4 from int to this bitfield struct; changed ft_80089890 to return UnkPlBonusBits* instead of UNK_T.
Expanded ft_800898B4_t from a mostly unknown trailing array into named offset fields and bitfields, including kb_applied1 and x10/x11 bit flags used by pl_8003D644.
Refined pl/types.h stale move table layout around xC94, xCB8, xCBC, xCC0, xCD4 and pl_804D6470_t fields x118, x11C, x120, x128, and x12C to support typed accesses in the matched code.
Deleted src/melee/pl/pl_0371.h and moved/declared its relevant prototypes in src/melee/pl/pl_040D.h, then updated includes across fighter and item files from pl_0371.h to pl_040D.h.
Corrected gm_8016AEDC return type from bool to u32 and gm_8016B204 prototype from unknown to bool, matching use sites in plbonuslib.
Updated GALE01 symbols.txt to split lbl_803BD1F4 into smaller string objects and add lbl_803BD204 and lbl_803BD224 as string symbols.
smart_moves: Typed the fighter damage/bonus metadata instead of leaving it as UNK_T/int blobs, allowing plbonuslib to match with clear bitfield reads such as x2_b7, x10_b0, x11_b2, and kb_applied1.
Used small inline helpers for repeated boolean patterns, especially team/self/null-attacker checks and ID range checks, which reduced noisy control flow while preserving match-sensitive structure.
Converted return signatures to the types implied by callers and comparisons: bool for predicates like pl_8003D60C/gm_8016B204 and u32 for gm_8016AEDC where the code compares against 0 and -2.
Kept offset-based placeholder naming where semantics were not proven, but upgraded layout enough to remove broad unknown arrays at important offsets.
Consolidated pl_0371 declarations into pl_040D.h rather than keeping a stale header for nearby player-library addresses.
Used the bot-reported match deltas as validation: 4 new matches in main/melee/pl/plbonuslib, including a 2304-byte function going from 0% to 100%.
decomp_lessons: Large bonus/stat routines often require cross-module type cleanup before the C body can match; here plbonuslib depended on fighter damage metadata, stale move table layout, game-mode signatures, item-kind checks, and player/team helpers.
When a function returns a pointer into a typed substructure, changing the accessor signature can unlock cleaner typed copies and field comparisons downstream.
Bitfield reconstruction is useful when the assembly tests individual bits in adjacent bytes/words; preserving offset names like x10_b0 and x11_b4 is acceptable when semantics are unknown.
For matching, bool-vs-int and u32-vs-bool return types matter because they influence comparisons, extensions, and branch generation.
Extracting repeated conditions as inline helpers can be a matching tactic, not just a readability improvement, if it recreates the compiler's desired expression boundaries.
Header cleanup should follow address ownership: if declarations belong to the 040D player range, prefer expanding pl_040D.h rather than maintaining a narrow/deleted pl_0371.h.
String symbol boundaries in symbols.txt can need adjustment when data objects were previously over-coalesced; splitting them into string-sized labels improves data accuracy.
naming_conventions: Unknown structs remain prefixed with Unk when semantics are not established, as with UnkPlBonusBits.
Offset-derived field names were retained for uncertain fields: x18d4, xCBC, xCC0, xCD4, x10_b0, x11_b4.
Known or suspected semantic fields may be named within otherwise unknown structs, e.g. kb_applied1 in ft_800898B4_t.
Local/anonymous functions in this address range use fn_ prefixes when no better name is known, e.g. fn_8003EE2C and fn_8004138C.
Game-action constants GA_Air and GA_Ground were preferred over raw 1/0 comparisons where evidence was strong from ftLib_800865CC use.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Added PAD_STACK(8) in pl_8003ED0C to satisfy stack layout.
Used explicit true/false returns in pl_8003D60C and helper predicates instead of integer literals.
Introduced inline helper functions for repeated condition shapes: gm_8016B168_and_same_team, unk_cond, between_A1_D0, match_item_kind, and gm_cond.
Changed pl_8003ED0C signature from three parameters to four parameters to match observed caller/assembly behavior.
Replaced broad UNK_T/UNK_SIZE_ARRAY fields with concrete structs and bitfields so generated loads/stores matched exact offsets.
Scoped item_gobj inside a block in fn_8003EE2C and converted kind normalization into match_item_kind to produce cleaner matched control flow.
Adjusted symbol map string sizes around .data:0x803BD1F4 through .data:0x803BD224 to reflect separate string objects.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 matched code increased to 31.88%, +0.11% and +4096 bytes.

decomp.dev bot listed 4 new matches in main/melee/pl/plbonuslib: pl_8003D644 (+2304 bytes, 0% to 100%), pl_8003ED0C (+288 bytes, 0% to 100%), fn_8003EE2C (+20 bytes, 98.23% to 100%), and pl_8004049C (+13 bytes, 96.81% to 100%).
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename UnkPlBonusBits fields to semantic bonus/hit flag names.
Check callers of pl_8003ED0C to confirm the meaning of the added third int parameter.
Search for later cleanup of ft_800898B4_t bitfields x10_b0 through x11_b4.
Investigate whether pl_804D6470_t fields x118, x11C, x120, x128, and x12C were later named based on bonus thresholds.
Look for PRs that further decompile the pl_040D address range now exposed in pl_040D.h.
